texto: Resolve to serve no more, and you are at once freed. I do not ask that you place hands upon the tyrant to topple him over, but simply that you support him no longer; then you will behold him, like a great Colossus whose pedestal has been pulled away, fall of his own weight and break in pieces.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Étienne de La Boétie: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “Resolve to serve no more, and you are at once freed. I do not ask that you place hands upon the t…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, anarchy, freedom pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e freedom.

frase_original: Resolve to serve no more, and you are at once freed. I do not ask that you place hands upon the tyrant to topple him over, but simply that you support him no longer; then you will behold him, like a great Colossus whose pedestal has been pulled away, fall of his own weight and break in pieces.
